result.note: KEYSTONE compound end-to-end render stack on ANIMATED 'classroom' (1920x1080, 8 frames, keyframe_every=4, bounces=12). Camera DOLLIED+PANNED+YAWED (cam_motion=1.0) for real screen-space motion + silhouette disocclusion. REFERENCE = every frame FULLY at 4096 spp, adaptive OFF, denoise OFF (true frames); T_ref = summed whole-subprocess wall-clock. OURS = keyframes rendered with the FULL anchor stack [adaptive sampling (thr=0.02, min=16) + oidn denoiser + albedo/normal prefiltered guides + light-tree many-light importance sampling, draft_spp=512] at full whole-subprocess wall-clock; non-key frames reprojected by Cycles motion vectors (our backward-gather warp) + OUR disocclusion mask [OOB|MV-divergence|depth-discontinuity|fwd/bwd-consistency, dilated], disocclusions handled by hole_fill=rerender. net_speedup = T_ref / T_stack = ONE measured wall-clock ratio on the SAME box (NOT a product of per-stage speedups). quality = end-to-end SSIM of DELIVERED frames vs TRUE frames (GLOBAL + per-8x8-tile worst/p5, tonemapped linear HDR) — real scikit-image on real pixels; SSIM is measurement-only (not charged to T_stack). Every keyframe/reference render TIME is real whole-subprocess wall-clock; every reprojected frame's numpy warp/mask/fill time is real measured wall-time. ONE MODELED STEP: the disocclusion crop re-render is NOT border-rendered per-frame; its cost is charged as fixed_overhead + disocc_frac*keyframe_pixel_trace, where fixed_overhead=1.885s is the MEASURED Blender start+.blend load+BVH cost a real crop pays in FULL (NOT scaled by area) and keyframe_pixel_trace = keyframe_wall - fixed_overhead. Honest and CONSERVATIVE (a resident-Blender pipeline amortizes the fixed overhead across crops) — NOT an upper bound. Because this crop-trace time is DERIVED (area-scaled from a measured full trace) it is the ONLY non-directly-measured number, so modeled=true. The disocclusion PATCH pixels are the ANCHOR-QUALITY render of that frame (adaptive+denoiser+guides+light-tree, draft_spp) — exactly what a crop render at the SAME anchor stack produces — NOT the 4096-spp reference, so the delivered-frame SSIM is a faithful measure of the composited pipeline (no patch scores SSIM~1 by construction). Each non-key frame's anchor render is a QUALITY/motion input only; its FULL cost is NOT charged (only the crop fraction is), so we render more pixels than the pipeline pays for purely to keep the patch quality honest.

KEYSTONE compound end-to-end render stack on ANIMATED 'classroom' (1920x1080, 8 frames, keyframe_every=6, bounces=12). Camera DOLLIED+PANNED+YAWED (cam_motion=1.0) for real screen-space motion + silhouette disocclusion. REFERENCE = every frame FULLY at 4096 spp, adaptive OFF, denoise OFF (true frames); T_ref = summed whole-subprocess wall-clock. OURS = keyframes rendered with the FULL anchor stack [adaptive sampling (thr=0.02, min=16) + oidn denoiser + albedo/normal prefiltered guides + light-tree many-light importance sampling, draft_spp=512] at full whole-subprocess wall-clock; non-key frames reprojected by Cycles motion vectors (our backward-gather warp) + OUR disocclusion mask [OOB|MV-divergence|depth-discontinuity|fwd/bwd-consistency, dilated], disocclusions handled by hole_fill=rerender. net_speedup = T_ref / T_stack = ONE measured wall-clock ratio on the SAME box (NOT a product of per-stage speedups). quality = end-to-end SSIM of DELIVERED frames vs TRUE frames (GLOBAL + per-8x8-tile worst/p5, tonemapped linear HDR) — real scikit-image on real pixels; SSIM is measurement-only (not charged to T_stack). Every keyframe/reference render TIME is real whole-subprocess wall-clock; every reprojected frame's numpy warp/mask/fill time is real measured wall-time. ONE MODELED STEP: the disocclusion crop re-render is NOT border-rendered per-frame; its cost is charged as fixed_overhead + disocc_frac*keyframe_pixel_trace, where fixed_overhead=1.841s is the MEASURED Blender start+.blend load+BVH cost a real crop pays in FULL (NOT scaled by area) and keyframe_pixel_trace = keyframe_wall - fixed_overhead. Honest and CONSERVATIVE (a resident-Blender pipeline amortizes the fixed overhead across crops) — NOT an upper bound. Because this crop-trace time is DERIVED (area-scaled from a measured full trace) it is the ONLY non-directly-measured number, so modeled=true. The disocclusion PATCH pixels are the ANCHOR-QUALITY render of that frame (adaptive+denoiser+guides+light-tree, draft_spp) — exactly what a crop render at the SAME anchor stack produces — NOT the 4096-spp reference, so the delivered-frame SSIM is a faithful measure of the composited pipeline (no patch scores SSIM~1 by construction). Each non-key frame's anchor render is a QUALITY/motion input only; its FULL cost is NOT charged (only the crop fraction is), so we render more pixels than the pipeline pays for purely to keep the patch quality honest.

REAL production Cycles CONVERGENCE test: scene='classroom' (1920x1080, bounces=12, seed=0). LEVER = faster convergence (LOSSLESS), NOT a denoiser: OURS = spp cap 512 + adaptive sampling (swept threshold, min=16) + light-tree (many-lights importance sampling), denoise OFF. REFERENCE = 4096 spp, adaptive OFF, light-tree OFF, guiding OFF, denoise OFF (pure unbiased ground truth). Every ours_render_s and ref_render_s is whole-subprocess wall-clock (launch+.blend load+BVH+trace+PNG encode, NO exclusions) on the SAME box/Blender/resolution/bounces/device/seed. SSIM (global + per-8x8-tile worst + p5) is real scikit-image on the two real rendered PNGs — per-tile catches any high-frequency degradation the global average would hide. net_speedup=ref/ours wall-clock. effective_samples is Blender's OWN reported samples (labelled per point); it is NEVER fabricated. Reference rendered ONCE on this box and cached by (scene,res,ref_spp,bounces,seed); ref_render_s is that real measured time. The deliverable is the samples-to-reference-SSIM FRONTIER in 'frontier'. NO WIN: best sweep point reached SSIM 0.8936 < target 0.98 (at 6.32x). Convergence levers did not fully reach the reference at this sample cap; raise 'samples' or lower the adaptive_threshold floor.

REAL production Cycles CONVERGENCE test: scene='classroom' (1920x1080, bounces=12, seed=0). LEVER = faster convergence (LOSSLESS), NOT a denoiser: OURS = spp cap 512 + adaptive sampling (swept threshold, min=16) (light-tree OFF), denoise OFF. REFERENCE = 4096 spp, adaptive OFF, light-tree OFF, guiding OFF, denoise OFF (pure unbiased ground truth). Every ours_render_s and ref_render_s is whole-subprocess wall-clock (launch+.blend load+BVH+trace+PNG encode, NO exclusions) on the SAME box/Blender/resolution/bounces/device/seed. SSIM (global + per-8x8-tile worst + p5) is real scikit-image on the two real rendered PNGs — per-tile catches any high-frequency degradation the global average would hide. net_speedup=ref/ours wall-clock. effective_samples is Blender's OWN reported samples (labelled per point); it is NEVER fabricated. Reference rendered ONCE on this box and cached by (scene,res,ref_spp,bounces,seed); ref_render_s is that real measured time (reused from cache this run, not re-paid). The deliverable is the samples-to-reference-SSIM FRONTIER in 'frontier'. NO WIN: best sweep point reached SSIM 0.8584 < target 0.98 (at 8.35x). Convergence levers did not fully reach the reference at this sample cap; raise 'samples' or lower the adaptive_threshold floor.

DISTRIBUTION / wall-clock-envelope lever on a REAL production Cycles scene 'classroom' (1920x1080, spp=512, bounces=12). ONE frame split into 8 tiles (grid 2x4); EACH tile is a real Cycles render_border subprocess at the SAME spp/bounces/seed (adaptive+denoise OFF), so the split is LOSSLESS. All times are real time.perf_counter() whole-subprocess wall-clock (launch+.blend load+BVH+border trace+PNG encode, NO exclusions) on the SAME box. T_serial=28.40s = SUM of tile times (what ONE GPU pays tile-by-tile). T_parallel_ideal=3.65s = MAX tile time (perfect N-GPU fan-out envelope). load_balance_efficiency=0.973 = mean/max tile time. net_speedup=7.79x is the IDEAL FAN-OUT CEILING (T_serial/T_parallel_ideal) — an UPPER BOUND, NOT an achievable production number: real cross-pod fan-out ALSO pays network scatter (ship the scene to N pods), network gather (pull N tile PNGs back), and scheduler/queue overhead, NONE of which is measured here (single box, zero-comms assumption; every tile assumed to start at t=0 on its own idle GPU). quality=1.0000 is REAL scikit-image SSIM of the reassembled composite vs a single full-frame render at the SAME spp (global + per-8x8-tile worst=0.9999/p5=1.0000); a lossless split should score ~1.0 and a low worst tile is a seam/border-rounding BUG we report, not hide. Composite pixel coverage=1.0000. sum-of-tiles/full-frame=1.719 (>1 == real per-tile launch/.blend-load/BVH overhead of the naive split). Full-frame reference rendered ONCE on this box and cached by (scene,res,spp,bounces,seed); full_frame_render_s is that real measured time.

DISTRIBUTION / wall-clock-envelope lever on a REAL production Cycles scene 'classroom' (1920x1080, spp=512, bounces=12). ONE frame split into 16 tiles (grid 4x4); EACH tile is a real Cycles render_border subprocess at the SAME spp/bounces/seed (adaptive+denoise OFF), so the split is LOSSLESS. All times are real time.perf_counter() whole-subprocess wall-clock (launch+.blend load+BVH+border trace+PNG encode, NO exclusions) on the SAME box. T_serial=41.51s = SUM of tile times (what ONE GPU pays tile-by-tile). T_parallel_ideal=2.75s = MAX tile time (perfect N-GPU fan-out envelope). load_balance_efficiency=0.944 = mean/max tile time. net_speedup=15.11x is the IDEAL FAN-OUT CEILING (T_serial/T_parallel_ideal) — an UPPER BOUND, NOT an achievable production number: real cross-pod fan-out ALSO pays network scatter (ship the scene to N pods), network gather (pull N tile PNGs back), and scheduler/queue overhead, NONE of which is measured here (single box, zero-comms assumption; every tile assumed to start at t=0 on its own idle GPU). quality=1.0000 is REAL scikit-image SSIM of the reassembled composite vs a single full-frame render at the SAME spp (global + per-8x8-tile worst=1.0000/p5=1.0000); a lossless split should score ~1.0 and a low worst tile is a seam/border-rounding BUG we report, not hide. Composite pixel coverage=1.0000. sum-of-tiles/full-frame=2.513 (>1 == real per-tile launch/.blend-load/BVH overhead of the naive split). Full-frame reference rendered ONCE on this box and cached by (scene,res,spp,bounces,seed); full_frame_render_s is that real measured time (reused from cache this run, not re-paid).
